question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition between the prune() deletion of transactions where consensusTimestamp <= ?1 and a concurrent read via findByConsensusTimestampBetween(), causing a transaction record to be silently dropped from query results mid-read, making a submitted transaction appear to have never existed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() uses a JPQL delete query 'delete from Transaction where consensusTimestamp <= ?1' without row-level locking, allowing a concurrent insert of a tampered transaction with a backdated consensusTimestamp to be immediately pruned before integrity checks occur, effectively erasing evidence of the tampered submission?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween(long startInclusive, long endInclusive, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user supply an extremely wide timestamp range (startInclusive=0, endInclusive=Long.MAX_VALUE) combined with a large Pageable page size to exhaust database memory and cause a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate submitted transactions from being queried?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween(long startInclusive, long endInclusive, Pageable pageable)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where startInclusive equals endInclusive at a boundary value (e.g., Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE) to trigger an integer overflow or unexpected BETWEEN clause behavior in the underlying SQL, causing incorrect transaction records to be returned and misrepresenting the state of submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Transaction.isNew() always returns true to force duplicate inserts of a transaction with the same consensusTimestamp (the @Id), causing a constraint violation that disrupts the importer pipeline and prevents legitimate submitted transactions from being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a crafted record-stream file, cause a Transaction entity with a manipulated transactionHash (byte[]) to be saved via save(), so that the stored hash no longer matches the actual transaction bytes, allowing the tampered transaction to pass hash-based lookups while containing falsified transfer data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a Transaction to be saved with a null transactionHash field (since the column is nullable in the schema), bypassing any downstream hash-integrity checks and making the submitted transaction unverifiable by external auditors querying the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the memo (byte[]) field of a submitted transaction before it is persisted via save(), injecting oversized or binary-crafted memo content that causes downstream REST API consumers to misparse or crash when deserializing the transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the result (Integer) field of a submitted Transaction before it is persisted via save(), changing a failed transaction result code to SUCCESS (22), causing the mirror node to report a fraudulent successful transaction to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the chargedTxFee (Long) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), setting it to zero or a negative value, causing the mirror node to report incorrect fee data that misleads users about the actual cost of submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the payerAccountId (EntityId) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), substituting a different account ID, causing the mirror node to attribute a transaction to the wrong payer and corrupting account-level transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the type (Integer) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), changing the transaction type (e.g., from CRYPTODELETE to CRYPTOTRANSFER), causing the mirror node to misclassify the transaction and hide destructive operations from monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the errata (ErrataType) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), setting it to a value that marks a legitimate transaction as an errata/correction, causing downstream consumers to ignore or override the original transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the scheduled (boolean) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), flipping it from false to true, causing the mirror node to misreport a non-scheduled transaction as scheduled and corrupting schedule execution audit trails?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the nonce (Integer) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), setting it to a value that collides with another transaction's nonce, causing duplicate-transaction detection logic to incorrectly suppress or merge distinct submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the validStartNs (Long) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), setting it to a future timestamp, causing the mirror node to store a transaction that appears valid in the future and bypasses time-based validity window checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the validDurationSeconds (Long) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), setting it to an extremely large value, causing the mirror node to report an impossibly long validity window that confuses downstream transaction-expiry logic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the nodeAccountId (EntityId) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), substituting a non-existent or attacker-controlled node account, causing the mirror node to misattribute transaction routing and corrupt node-level analytics?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (inherited from CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the itemizedTransfer (List<ItemizedTransfer>, stored as JSONB) field of a submitted Transaction before save(), injecting malformed JSON that causes a deserialization error in downstream REST API consumers reading the transaction record?
